title: Effect of Bougie Use on First-Attempt Success in Tracheal Intubations: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

1.reason: Identified as systematic review/meta-analysis in title section.

2.reason: Structured abstract with background, methods, results, conclusion provided.

3.reason: Background and Importance sections describe rationale in context of existing knowledge.

4.reason: Goals of This Investigation explicitly states the objective.

5.reason: Detailed eligibility criteria described, including subgroup planning.

6.reason: Lists all databases searched with last search date June 15, 2023.

7.reason: Full search strategies provided in Appendix E1.

8.reason: Two independent reviewers screened titles/abstracts and full texts with consensus.

9.reason: Standardized form, one extractor with second cross-checking, consensus for disagreements.

10a.reason: Primary and secondary outcomes listed and defined.

10b.reason: Variables extracted are listed (setting, RSI, age, sex, etc.).

11.reason: RoB 2 for RCTs and modified Newcastle-Ottawa for observational; one reviewer cross-checked by methodologist.

12.reason: Risk ratios with 95% CIs specified as effect measures.

13a.reason: Eligibility criteria and subgroup planning described.

13b.reason: No description of handling missing summary statistics or data conversions.

13c.reason: Forest plots, tables, and funnel plots described.

13d.reason: Random-effects DerSimonian-Laird model, I2 for heterogeneity, Review Manager 5.4.1 specified.

13e.reason: Subgroup analyses described to explore heterogeneity.

13f.reason: Sensitivity analyses excluding outlier studies described.

14.reason: Funnel plot described to assess publication bias.

16a.reason: Flow diagram (Figure 1) with screening numbers reported.

16b.reason: Excluded studies after full-text review not individually cited with reasons.

17.reason: Table 1 presents characteristics of each included study.

18.reason: Risk of bias assessments presented in Appendix E3 and summarized.

19.reason: Forest plots show individual study results with effect estimates and CIs.

20a.reason: Characteristics and risk of bias of contributing studies summarized.

20b.reason: Pooled estimates with 95% CIs and I2 reported.

20c.reason: Subgroup analyses results presented in Table 2 and Appendix E5.

20d.reason: Sensitivity analysis excluding BOUGIE trial reported.

21.reason: Funnel plot asymmetry assessment reported in Appendix E4.

23a.reason: Discussion compares findings with previous reviews.

23b.reason: Limitations of evidence including risk of bias and heterogeneity discussed.

23c.reason: Limitations of review process noted (eg, inability to do experience subgroup).

23d.reason: Implications for practice and future research discussed.

24b.reason: Protocol registered on PROSPERO; protocol available on request.

24c.reason: Post hoc analysis of difficult airway subgroup explicitly noted as amendment.

26.reason: Authors report no conflicts of interest.

27.reason: Data sharing statement: data, protocol, code available on request.

item_2.reason: Objective stated: evaluate evidence on bougie use and first-attempt intubation success.

item_3.reason: Inclusion/exclusion described: adults, bougie vs without, RCTs and comparative observational; manikin/cadaver excluded.

item_4.reason: Lists Ovid Cochrane Central, Embase, Medline, Scopus, Web of Science, searched to June 2023.

item_5.reason: No risk of bias assessment tool mentioned; GRADE assesses certainty, not study-level risk of bias.

item_6.reason: Random-effects meta-analysis and GRADE for certainty described.

item_8.reason: Pooled RR 1.11 (95% CI 1.06-1.17) reported with subgroup estimates.

item_10.reason: Suggests bougie should be used first, not as rescue device.

title: Efficacy of prescribed opioids for acute pain after being discharged from the emergency department: A systematic review and meta-analysis.

1.reason: Title identifies as 'systematic review and meta-analysis'.

2.reason: Structured abstract with background, methods, results, conclusions provided.

3.reason: Introduction describes existing knowledge gaps and rationale for review.

4.reason: Explicit objective stated to synthesize evidence on prescribed opioids vs nonopioids for ED-discharged patients.

5.reason: Eligibility criteria explicitly described including inclusion/exclusion.

6.reason: Databases listed (MEDLINE, EMBASE, CINAHL, PsycINFO, CENTRAL, gray literature) with search dates.

7.reason: Search strategy themes described and full strategies in Supplement Methods S1.

8.reason: Two independent reviewers screened with third reviewer for conflicts; Covidence used.

9.reason: Two reviewers extracted data independently with conflict resolution; authors contacted.

10b.reason: Variables extracted listed (sample size, age, criteria, treatment types, outcomes).

11.reason: EPOC tool used by two reviewers independently with third for conflicts.

12.reason: SMD for efficacy and OR for adverse events specified.

13a.reason: Describes pooling when ≥3 studies available for outcomes.

13b.reason: Data conversion methods (percentages to SMD) described.

13d.reason: Random-effects model, inverse variance, heterogeneity measures (I², tau², Q), software (RevMan) reported.

13e.reason: Subgroup analyses (codeine vs other, pediatric vs adult) described.

13f.reason: Multiple sensitivity analyses described (short-term, excluding high RoB, excluding placebo).

14.reason: States no publication bias tests done due to <10 studies; no other reporting bias methods described.

15.reason: No GRADE or certainty assessment described.

16a.reason: PRISMA flow diagram (Figure 1) and search numbers reported.

17.reason: Table 1 presents characteristics of each included study.

18.reason: Risk of bias presented in Supplement Figure S1 with summary in text.

19.reason: Table 2 presents results for each study.

20a.reason: Discussion summarizes characteristics and risk of bias of contributing studies.

20b.reason: Pooled SMDs/ORs with 95% CIs and I² presented.

20d.reason: Sensitivity analysis results presented in text and supplements.

23a.reason: Discussion compares findings with previous reviews.

23b.reason: Limitations of evidence (heterogeneity, exclusion criteria, external validity) discussed.

23c.reason: Limitations of review process discussed in Limitations section.

23d.reason: Implications for practice and future research discussed.

25.reason: Funding source and role of funder reported.

27.reason: No statement on availability of data extraction forms, code, or analytical data.

item_3.reason: RCTs on prescribed opioids for ED-discharged patients specified

item_4.reason: Lists MEDLINE, EMBASE, CINAHL, PsycINFO, CENTRAL, gray literature, searched to January 2023

title: Intubation Rates following Prehospital Administration of Ketamine for Acute Agitation: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

4.reason: Explicit primary and secondary aims stated

5.reason: Inclusion and exclusion criteria explicitly listed

6.reason: Ten databases plus Google Scholar listed with last search date 13/Feb/2022

7.reason: Search filter referenced and Appendix A provides complete search strategy

8.reason: Two independent reviewers (CL, AO) screened with kappa, third resolved disagreements; Covidence used

9.reason: One author extracted, 10% double-checked by another; authors contacted for missing data

10a.reason: Primary outcomes (prehospital and ED intubations) and additional outcomes listed

10b.reason: Other variables listed (dose, setting, methodology, agitation score, etc.)

11.reason: Newcastle-Ottawa scale, two independent authors with consensus described

13f.reason: Leave-one-out and alternative back-transformation sensitivity analyses described

14.reason: Publication bias not assessed; stated funnel plots inaccurate but no alternative method used

15.reason: GRADE tool described with two independent assessors

16b.reason: Friedman et al. cited as appearing eligible but excluded with reason

17.reason: Table 1 lists each included study with characteristics

19.reason: Table 2 presents intubation counts and percentages per study

20a.reason: Characteristics and risk of bias of contributing studies summarized

20b.reason: Pooled estimates with 95% CI and I2 reported for each synthesis

20c.reason: Subgroup analysis of outliers presented in Figure 5

20d.reason: Leave-one-out results reported (no individually influential study)

23a.reason: Discussion contextualizes findings with prior ED ketamine literature

23b.reason: Limitations of evidence (observational, heterogeneity, dose accuracy) discussed

23d.reason: Implications for practice and future research provided

27.reason: States data extracted are publicly available from respective journal articles

23b.reason: Limitations of evidence (high bias, heterogeneity) discussed.
